title: On the Cyclic (910,405,180) Difference-Set Problem
lean_gate_reason: Lean would only formalize supporting quotient-profile lemmas. The blocking gap is earlier and mathematical: the exact cyclic row is still unresolved, and formalization would not convert the current packet into a publishable theorem packet.
micro_paper_assessment: The exact row remains a strong micro-paper target if closed, but the current audited packet is still only a supporting quotient-profile slice and is not near-paper.
family_affinity: strong classical multiplier-and-contraction lane for cyclic difference sets
generalization_signal: moderate; the prime-index and composite-quotient contraction method should transfer to nearby rows with odd normalized multipliers
strongest_honest_claim: If a cyclic (910,405,180) difference set exists, then its contractions to C_5, C_7, C_13, and C_35 lie in the exact finite profile lists verified in the record. This is stronger than an example, but it is still a supporting structural slice rather than the title theorem of a publishable note.
paper_title_hint: Quotient-Profile Constraints for a Hypothetical Cyclic (910,405,180) Difference Set
candidate_theorem_slice: Any cyclic (910,405,180) difference set would force exact quotient profiles on C_5, C_7, C_13, and C_35. The current packet does not justify a standalone publishable theorem slice beyond those supporting restrictions.
publication_packet_quality: supporting structural slice only; not near-paper
next_action: Do not promote this packet. Preserve the corrected quotient-profile lemmas, retire the invalid unique-C_65 corridor claim, and either run one more bounded same-lane compatibility check with a strict close-or-cool stop condition or cool the slug as a not-yet-publishable variant.
